item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's a lot of potential here, but I feel like any anomaly capable of comprehending and altering a wide variety of sophisticated legal documents would be a bit more formal and well-spoken.

I certainly won't downvote on that basis, because that's just my personal sense of how an anomaly of this type would behave. Still, ultimately there just wasn't enough weirdness described here to win my upvote. I think the anomaly's main property is sufficiently weird, so a longer and more historically thorough log of its identified historical effects could easily maintain the fun tone while adding more variety.

The extended concern about the earthquake was just not very interesting. It would have been okay as a brief addendum to get the reader to crack a smile one last time, but it went on for far too long; there was no successful development of mounting tension, and the humor of it wore thin quickly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The anomaly at play here is really intriguing; I was immensely disappointed when it only showed up in any unique capacity in a single list of events. The majority of the piece uses the effect in a way which isn't at all interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You got a laugh out of me. That poor containment specialist. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Goddamn, when I first opened this I was confused by the header- but when I scrolled back up to +1 I reread it and had a chuckle. Very funny and interesting article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

I live not to far from Laguna Beach and it was a hoot to see it used in this fashion (as it’s always been a strong artist community)

Anyway, this was a very fun read with an interesting anomaly (I don’t think I’ve seen something similar) and best bit was the humorous ending.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to swordlover87 and Discrord user change name#7226 for critiquing.

This article came about as wanting to subvert the idea of an extremely hostile statue: SCP-5114 doesn't attack physically, but can hurt you in other ways. Also, he/she isn't hostile due to hating humans, rather than just being sensitive.

Originally I had the intention of ending the Addendum with SCP-5114 going all 'oh, you want me to list some names? i don't see a contract with my name on it.' It would've been the ultimate burn, now that I think about it, but it would've been rather stupid of SCP-5114. If it did that, then the Foundation likely wouldn't bother relocating it another time if the need arose.

Of course, there's the possibility that SCP-5114 does have access to the contact information of a hundred Insurgents and 25 anartists, but has decided to save them up if he needs more 'leverage' later.

The image for this article is from Wikimedia Commons: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Back_side-_BEATA_ROSTAS_Exile_Sz%C3%A1m%C5%B1z%C3%B6ttek,_Cyprus,_Agia_Napa,_Sculpture_Park_Ayia_Napa_,_limestone,_250x_180x_90_cm.jpg
